topic: Practical OpenAI and ChatGPT usage patterns, real use cases

entries.q: What is the OpenAI Cookbook and where can I access it
entries.a: The OpenAI Cookbook is a resource that provides example code and guides for accomplishing common tasks with the OpenAI API, and it can be accessed at cookbook.openai.com

entries.q: What do I need to run the examples in the OpenAI Cookbook
entries.a: To run the examples in the OpenAI Cookbook, you need an OpenAI account and an associated API key

entries.q: How can I set my OpenAI API key as an environment variable
entries.a: You can set your OpenAI API key as an environment variable by creating an environment variable called OPENAI_API_KEY with your API key, or by creating a .env file at the root of your repository with OPENAI_API_KEY=<your API key>

entries.q: What programming language are most code examples in the OpenAI Cookbook written in
entries.a: Most code examples in the OpenAI Cookbook are written in Python

entries.q: Can the concepts in the OpenAI Cookbook be applied in languages other than Python
entries.a: Yes, the concepts in the OpenAI Cookbook can be applied in any programming language

entries.q: What type of license does the OpenAI Cookbook use
entries.a: The OpenAI Cookbook uses the MIT License

entries.q: Where can I find related resources for learning about OpenAI and the OpenAI API
entries.a: Related resources for learning about OpenAI and the OpenAI API can be found at cookbook.openai.com/related_resources

entries.q: How do I create a free OpenAI account to get started with the OpenAI API
entries.a: You can create a free OpenAI account by signing up at platform.openai.com/signup
